prompt: Build a Field Service Membership & Data Governance system that supports secure offline account access, member lifecycle management, searchable records, auditable activity tracking, offline data cleansing, and address-based service eligibility checks. The interface, rendered with HTMX-driven form workflows, guides three roles through role-based home views: Member Desk users create and maintain member profiles with status, tags, and organization links, and run guided flows for join, renew, upgrade, downgrade, deactivate, or cancel, with immediate inline validation and a timeline showing what changed, by whom, and when in MM/DD/YYYY 12-hour format. Dispatch users manage member and site address books, select locations through manual pin placement on an offline floorplan-style grid or direct coordinate entry, and validate whether requests fall within supported service areas based on administrative region rules or a maximum dispatch radius of 25 miles by default. Administrators oversee account controls, trusted devices, anomaly monitoring, SLA consoles, cleansing templates, and search configuration, including trending searches and no-result recommendations that suggest nearby tags or recent members. Search is available from every screen with full-text matching, keyword highlighting, filters by tags, categories, and time ranges, plus configurable trending search panels. The backend is implemented in Flask, exposing REST-style endpoints consumed by HTMX partial requests to keep workflows modular while enforcing server-side validation and consistent state transitions. SQLite provides local persistence with transactional guarantees for member records, address book entries, service areas, audit logs, cleansing templates, template versions, device records, and anomaly flags. Authentication is strictly offline with locally verified username and password, strong adaptive password hashing, 15-minute lockout after 5 failed attempts, admin-controlled freeze/deactivate actions, trusted device management limited to 5 devices per user, and re-authentication for sensitive actions after 30 minutes of inactivity. Write operations are atomic, conflicting edits are rejected through optimistic concurrency, and the UI shows a 60-second edit lock warning. Operational auditing records logins, permission changes, all create/read/update/delete events, and exports with timestamps and acting user identities; administrators can search logs, flag anomalies such as more than 50 record reads in 10 minutes, and monitor local SLA targets like member search returning results within 2 seconds for 50,000 records using an on-screen alert console. The data cleansing pipeline runs fully offline on imported CSV files, supporting field mapping, missing-value rules, configurable deduplication thresholds, outlier flags, standardized USD and imperial units, normalized date-time formats, and place-name standardization through a locally maintained reference table. Cleansing templates are reusable and versioned, sensitive fields are masked in the interface, and encrypted at rest within the SQLite database using an application-managed encryption key stored on the host machine.
